gaps.issue: WorkspaceSwitcher.vue, SettingsPanel.vue, and AgentManager.vue still use hardcoded rgba(74, 158, 255, ...) — 3 non-MessageCard components. Should use var(--tool-accent-bg) / var(--tool-accent-border).
gaps.note: Out of iteration scope — MessageCard.vue is fully tokenized

gaps.issue: Code block syntax highlighting (hljs) uses hardcoded colors in theme.css. Intentionally separate from the design token system.
gaps.note: Intentional — hljs themes are a separate concern

gaps.issue: No timestamps on messages — conversation lacks temporal anchoring.
gaps.note: Deferred — requires data model changes beyond CSS scope

gaps.issue: Avatar font-size uses hardcoded 13px instead of var(--text-sm). Trivial fix.

details: ✅ Both light and dark mode variants defined in theme.css
✅ Zero raw 0.15s/0.2s/0.3s durations remaining in MessageCard.vue
✅ var(--tool-accent-border) used for image hover shadow
✅ No hardcoded accent color in image hover

afterIteration5.remainingIssues: Hardcoded accent colors in 3 secondary components, hljs theme colors (intentional), no timestamps (deferred), avatar 13px (trivial)

qualityAssessment.improvement: +3.2 points — design token migration complete for MessageCard.vue

rationale: Gap score 10.4 is well below the 20 threshold. All four design-5 changes verified in source code. MessageCard.vue has zero hardcoded accent colors, zero raw transition durations, and fully theme-aware error states. The design token system (semantic colors, typography scale, border-radius tiers, transition timing) is comprehensive across light and dark modes. Remaining gaps are out of CSS scope (timestamps), intentionally separate (hljs), in secondary components, or trivial (avatar font-size). The core conversation UI has achieved Notion-level design system maturity.
